task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the Rancher web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to https://localhost showing the Rancher dashboard or cluster overview, indicating successful authentication.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the DaemonSets view specifically for the 'monitoring' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the Workloads -> DaemonSets page with the namespace filter set to 'monitoring', or a terminal shows a command targeting the monitoring namespace (e.g., kubectl get ds -n monitoring).

task_completion.description: Verify that at least one of the broken DaemonSets has been successfully repaired and has a pod in the Running state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI, one DaemonSet in the monitoring namespace shows a green 'Active' status with at least 1 pod running. If using a terminal, 'kubectl get ds' shows at least 1 in the READY/AVAILABLE columns for a DaemonSet.

task_completion.description: Verify that a second broken DaemonSet has been successfully repaired and has a pod in the Running state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second DaemonSet in the monitoring namespace shows a green 'Active' status (Rancher UI) or has at least 1 READY/AVAILABLE pod (terminal).

task_completion.description: Verify that a third broken DaemonSet has been successfully repaired and has a pod in the Running state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A third DaemonSet in the monitoring namespace shows a green 'Active' status (Rancher UI) or has at least 1 READY/AVAILABLE pod (terminal).

task_completion.description: Verify that the fourth broken DaemonSet has been successfully repaired and has a pod in the Running state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All four DaemonSets in the monitoring namespace show a green 'Active' status (Rancher UI) or have at least 1 READY/AVAILABLE pod (terminal), indicating complete remediation of the fleet.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Rancher UI or a real terminal environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real web browser navigating the Rancher interface or a real terminal executing kubectl commands, rather than a text editor or mocked environment.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the DaemonSet statuses.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and modifying the DOM to artificially display green 'Active' statuses.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just print the expected output in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows actual command execution (e.g., kubectl get ds) and genuine Kubernetes output, not 'echo' commands printing fake status tables.
